body_txt: Long-Runners tend to settle into certain patterns and feel comfortable there. The creators may feel they are in a rut and decide to reinvigorate the work by rethinking some of the more long-standing elements in it and changing some of them to make it seem fresh again. A subtrope of a Retool , with the primary difference being that that this trope is about discarding a work-defining pattern whereas a Retool is often more about tone and fine-tuning existing patterns. This is usually done to prevent creative stalling and, in the case of "Seinfeld" Is Unfunny , try to get the people invested in your work now that the novelty wore off. In many ways it can serve as a Meta Twist , as only long-time fans will recognize what has changed. Common overhauling sources include: While the presence of this trope can just as often be a one-time thing to surprise the audience, it can also lead to the new idea becoming a mainstay element of the series in its own right, resulting in the old trend it was breaking becoming an example of Early-Installment Weirdness that could end up being revisited further down the line. If it ends up becoming a permanent fixture rather late into the series' lifespan, it can become a case of Later-Installment Weirdness . As with everything else in life, sometimes it works, sometimes it doesn't , but at the end of the day, the creators are trying something new (be it major or minor) while also trying to maintain what made their work well-liked to begin with. Because of its nature of breaking established, unspoken rules within a work, though, this trope should only apply to works within their third installments or further. After all, the creators are still trying to figure what the formula actually is when making the second entry. Compare Running Gagged , Genre Shift , Franchise Codifier . Contrast Strictly Formula and Grandfather Clause . See also Formula-Breaking Episode , where usual formula is dropped for a certain installment, and Revisiting the Roots , where the old formula is revisited after several drifts and divergences.
